docs.title: Paris ! (fr_01) - Script
docs.text:
Quest Index - Language: english - french - polish - italian

Educators & Designers: help improving this quest!

Comments and feedback: discuss in the Forum Improve translations: comment the Google Sheet Improve the script: propose an edit here

docs.title: init
docs.text:
// FR_01 PARIS
// Cards:
// - tour_eiffell (landmark education)
// - mr_eiffel (historical figure)
// - tour_eiffell_ticket (cultural practice)
// - tour_eiffell_map (navigation aid)
// - louvre (landmark education)
// - louvre_inside (cultural immersion)
// - monalisa (art education)
// - leaonardodavinci (historical figure)
// - liberty_leading (art education)
// - venusmilo (art education)
// - notredame (landmark education)
// - notredame_fire (recent history)
// - baguette (cultural food)
// - baguette_flour (ingredient education)
// - baguette_water (ingredient education)
// - baguette_yeast (ingredient education)
// - baguette_salt (ingredient education)
// Tasks:
// - Collect 4 baguette ingredients from various locations
// - Reach Monalisa
// Activities:
// - puzzle - Eiffel Tower
// Words: 
// ticket, coin, baguette, flour, water, yeast, salt, map, elevator, iron, museum, cathedral, painting, sculpture,
tags:
type: panel
color: red
---
<<set $TOTAL_COINS = 0>>
<<set $COLLECTED_ITEMS = 0>>
<<declare $QUEST_ITEMS = 0>>
<<declare $MET_GUIDE = false>>
<<declare $MET_MAJOR = false>>
<<declare $MET_MONALISA = false>>

Welcome to Paris! #line:fr01_start 
Go and talk with the tutor! #line:fr01_start_2
<<target tutor>>

docs.title: talk_tutor
docs.text:
actor: tutor
color: blue
---
I saw Antura going to the Eiffel Tower. #line:fr01_talk_tutor
<<camera_focus tour_eiffell>>
Follow the target or use the map! #line:fr01_talk_tutor_2 
Get there as soon as possible! #line:fr01_talk_tutor_3

docs.title: talk_louvre_external
docs.text:
group: louvre
actor: OLD_WOMAN
---
<<if $MET_MONALISA>>
    <<jump go_bakery>>
<<else>>
<<asset louvre>>
This is the entrance to the Louvre, our national art museum. #line:0cf1cc8 
Do you want to enter? #line:0f74ff9
-> Yes: #line:090114f 
    Enjoy your visit! #line:056e051 
    <<action AREA_LOUVRE_ENTER >>
-> No: #line:077422a 
    Okay. #line:0c28ea0 
<<endif>>

docs.title: talk_eiffell_roof
docs.text:
group: toureiffel
tags: actor=GUIDE
---
<<asset toureiffell>>
The Eiffel Tower is 300 meters tall. #line:08c1973 
<<asset mr_eiffel>>
Built by Mr Eiffel in 1887. #line:09e5c3b 
<<asset iron>>
It's made of iron! #line:0d59ade 
I saw Antura going towards Notre Dame. #line:04d1e52 
<<camera_focus notredame>>
Get there! #line:083b3bf

docs.title: talk_eiffell_guide
docs.text:
group: toureiffel
actor: guide
---
<<if $TOTAL_COINS > 2>>
    Here is your ticket. #line:04e74ad 
    <<asset tour_eiffell_ticket>>
    <<set $TOTAL_COINS = $TOTAL_COINS-3>>
    <<action area_toureiffel>>
    <<asset tour_eiffell_map>>
    I saw Antura going up to the top of the tower. #line:089abda 
    Take the elevator! #line:0585a5e 
<<elseif $TOTAL_COINS > 0>> 
    Collect all coins! #line:04d966b 
<<else>>
    The ticket for the Eiffel Tower costs 3 coins. #line:069cbb3 
<<endif>>

docs.title: talk_notre_dame
docs.text:
group: notredame
tags: actor=OLD_MAN, asset=notredame
---
I'm the Mayor of Paris. #line:0cc11fa 
This is Notre-Dame Cathedral. #line:06f3fa2 
<<asset notredame>>
It is a famous Gothic church, built in 1182. #line:02edc0f 
<<action AREA_NOTREDAME_ROOF>>
Come with me up to the roof of the church! #line:083dfcc 
<<set $MET_MAJOR = true>>

docs.title: talk_cook
docs.text:
group: bakery
actor: CRAZY_MAN
---
<<if $COLLECTED_ITEMS >= 4>>
    GREAT! I can now bake the Baguette. And... #line:0017917 
    CONGRATULATIONS! You won the game! Did you like it? #line:0d11596 
    <<quest_end 3>>
<<else>>
    Help me! Antura made a mess in my kitchen! #line:07bbb10 
    I can't find the ingredients to make the baguette. #line:09e867c 
    <<asset  baguette>>
    Our special French bread! #line:0874503 
    <<set $QUEST_ITEMS = 4>>
    Please bring me 4 ingredients: #line:07d64c7 
    flour, water, yeast, and salt. #line:0c01530 
<<endif>>

docs.title: visit_louvre
docs.text:
group: louvre
tags: actor=CRAZY_WOMAN
---
<<asset louvre_inside>>
You can find many sculptures and paintings. #line:08dc97f 
<<jump find_monalisa>>

docs.title: find_monalisa
docs.text:
group: louvre
tags: actor=CRAZY_WOMAN
---

<<action monalisa>>
Go and find the Mona Lisa! #line:0442392

docs.title: go_bakery
docs.text:
group: louvre
tags: actor=OLD_WOMAN
---

Now look for Antura! It went to the bakery for a baguette! #line:076ef0f 
Hurry up! #line:0e9c3e7

docs.title: baguette_salt
docs.text:
group: bakery
color: yellow
tags: actor=CRAZY_MAN
---
<<action COLLECT_1>>
<<card baguette_salt>>
This is salt. #line:00f1d2f

docs.title: baguette_flour
docs.text:
group: bakery
color: yellow
tags: actor=CRAZY_MAN
---
<<action COLLECT_2>>
<<card baguette_flour>>
This is flour. #line:06022b0

docs.title: baguette_water
docs.text:
group: bakery
color: yellow
tags: actor=CRAZY_MAN
---
<<action COLLECT_3>>
<<card baguette_water>>
This is water. #line:0c4d1f6

docs.title: baguette_yeast
docs.text:
group: bakery
color: yellow
tags: actor=CRAZY_MAN
---
<<action COLLECT_4>>
<<card baguette_yeast>>
This is yeast. #line:025865d

docs.title: talk_notre_dame_roof
docs.text:
group: notredame
tags: actor=OLD_MAN
---
<<asset notredame_fire>>
There was a big fire in 2019, but we could repair it. #line:09a0ead 
I saw Antura run into the Louvre Museum. #line:02ba888 
It is just across the River Seine. #line:00d22e5

docs.title: talk_louvre_guide
docs.text:
group: louvre
tags: actor=CRAZY_WOMAN
color: blue
---
Welcome to the Louvre Museum. What do you want to do? #line:0e6d2a5 
-> Tell me about the Louvre #line:0a5fc63 
    <<jump visit_louvre>>
-> Exit #line:0efc18f 
    <<if $MET_MONALISA>>
        <<action AREA_LOUVRE_EXIT>>
        Come back! #line:07dd921 
    <<else>>
        <<jump find_monalisa>>
    <<endif>>

docs.title: louvre_monalisa
docs.text:
group: louvre
color: yellow
tags: actor=CRAZY_WOMAN
---
<<asset monalisa>>

by the artist and scientist Leonardo da Vinci. #line:louvre_monalisa_3

docs.text:
group: louvre
color: yellow
tags: actor=CRAZY_WOMAN
---

by the French artist Eugène Delacroix #line:louvre_liberty_3

color: yellow
tags: actor=CRAZY_WOMAN,

docs.title: npc_woman
docs.text:
tags: actor=WOMAN
color: purple
---
Ciao. What do you want to know? #line:0070084 
-> What is the Eiffel Tower? #line:0d91dc0 
    The famous iron tower, 300 meters tall. #line:0f17af0 
    The symbol of Paris! #line:07a113f 
-> Where are we? #line:09dd1da 
    We are in Paris, the city of love! #line:02b627d 
-> Is this place real? #line:08bede4 
    Sure! Why are you asking? #line:08654e6 
    Well... it looks like a video game, doesn't it? #line:0bc62a3 
-> Nothing. Bye. #line:0fe0732

docs.title: npc_man
docs.text:
tags: actor=MAN
color: purple
---
Do you have any questions? #line:07b94e9 
-> Have you seen Antura? #line:0f18ad3 
    => Yes! Talk to everybody and follow the lights! #line:0cf9b4e 
    => No. Who is Antura? #line:0f9dd62 
-> What are you doing? #line:002796f 
    => I'm going to work! #line:0fe4ff4 
    => I'm going to buy bread at the bakery. #line:05a38a8 
-> Where do you come from? #line:05eabcf 
    => I wasn't born in this country. #line:0635a6a 
    => From planet Earth. #line:0749690 
-> Goodbye #line:0ee51fc

=> Hi! #line:0c4d9e4

docs.title: init
docs.text:
// FR_01 PARIS
// Cards:
// - tour_eiffell (landmark education)
// - mr_eiffel (historical figure)
// - tour_eiffell_ticket (cultural practice)
// - tour_eiffell_map (navigation aid)
// - louvre (landmark education)
// - louvre_inside (cultural immersion)
// - monalisa (art education)
// - leaonardodavinci (historical figure)
// - liberty_leading (art education)
// - venusmilo (art education)
// - notredame (landmark education)
// - notredame_fire (recent history)
// - baguette (cultural food)
// - baguette_flour (ingredient education)
// - baguette_water (ingredient education)
// - baguette_yeast (ingredient education)
// - baguette_salt (ingredient education)
// Tasks:
// - Collect 4 baguette ingredients from various locations
// - Reach Monalisa
// Activities:
// - puzzle - Eiffel Tower
// Words: 
// ticket, coin, baguette, flour, water, yeast, salt, map, elevator, iron, museum, cathedral, painting, sculpture,
tags:
type: panel
color: red
---
<<set $TOTAL_COINS = 0>>
<<set $COLLECTED_ITEMS = 0>>
<<declare $QUEST_ITEMS = 0>>
<<declare $MET_GUIDE = false>>
<<declare $MET_MAJOR = false>>
<<declare $MET_MONALISA = false>>

Welcome to Paris! #line:fr01_start 
Go and talk with the tutor! #line:fr01_start_2
<<target tutor>>

docs.title: talk_tutor
docs.text:
actor: tutor
color: blue
---
I saw Antura going to the Eiffel Tower. #line:fr01_talk_tutor
<<camera_focus tour_eiffell>>
Follow the target or use the map! #line:fr01_talk_tutor_2 
Get there as soon as possible! #line:fr01_talk_tutor_3

docs.title: talk_louvre_external
docs.text:
group: louvre
actor: OLD_WOMAN
---
<<if $MET_MONALISA>>
    <<jump go_bakery>>
<<else>>
<<asset louvre>>
This is the entrance to the Louvre, our national art museum. #line:0cf1cc8 
Do you want to enter? #line:0f74ff9
-> Yes: #line:090114f 
    Enjoy your visit! #line:056e051 
    <<action AREA_LOUVRE_ENTER >>
-> No: #line:077422a 
    Okay. #line:0c28ea0 
<<endif>>

docs.title: talk_eiffell_roof
docs.text:
group: toureiffel
tags: actor=GUIDE
---
<<asset toureiffell>>
The Eiffel Tower is 300 meters tall. #line:08c1973 
<<asset mr_eiffel>>
Built by Mr Eiffel in 1887. #line:09e5c3b 
<<asset iron>>
It's made of iron! #line:0d59ade 
I saw Antura going towards Notre Dame. #line:04d1e52 
<<camera_focus notredame>>
Get there! #line:083b3bf

docs.title: talk_eiffell_guide
docs.text:
group: toureiffel
actor: guide
---
<<if $TOTAL_COINS > 2>>
    Here is your ticket. #line:04e74ad 
    <<asset tour_eiffell_ticket>>
    <<set $TOTAL_COINS = $TOTAL_COINS-3>>
    <<action area_toureiffel>>
    <<asset tour_eiffell_map>>
    I saw Antura going up to the top of the tower. #line:089abda 
    Take the elevator! #line:0585a5e 
<<elseif $TOTAL_COINS > 0>> 
    Collect all coins! #line:04d966b 
<<else>>
    The ticket for the Eiffel Tower costs 3 coins. #line:069cbb3 
<<endif>>

docs.title: talk_notre_dame
docs.text:
group: notredame
tags: actor=OLD_MAN, asset=notredame
---
I'm the Mayor of Paris. #line:0cc11fa 
This is Notre-Dame Cathedral. #line:06f3fa2 
<<asset notredame>>
It is a famous Gothic church, built in 1182. #line:02edc0f 
<<action AREA_NOTREDAME_ROOF>>
Come with me up to the roof of the church! #line:083dfcc 
<<set $MET_MAJOR = true>>

docs.title: talk_cook
docs.text:
group: bakery
actor: CRAZY_MAN
---
<<if $COLLECTED_ITEMS >= 4>>
    GREAT! I can now bake the Baguette. And... #line:0017917 
    CONGRATULATIONS! You won the game! Did you like it? #line:0d11596 
    <<quest_end 3>>
<<else>>
    Help me! Antura made a mess in my kitchen! #line:07bbb10 
    I can't find the ingredients to make the baguette. #line:09e867c 
    <<asset  baguette>>
    Our special French bread! #line:0874503 
    <<set $QUEST_ITEMS = 4>>
    Please bring me 4 ingredients: #line:07d64c7 
    flour, water, yeast, and salt. #line:0c01530 
<<endif>>

docs.title: visit_louvre
docs.text:
group: louvre
tags: actor=CRAZY_WOMAN
---
<<asset louvre_inside>>
You can find many sculptures and paintings. #line:08dc97f 
<<jump find_monalisa>>

docs.title: find_monalisa
docs.text:
group: louvre
tags: actor=CRAZY_WOMAN
---

<<action monalisa>>
Go and find the Mona Lisa! #line:0442392

docs.title: go_bakery
docs.text:
group: louvre
tags: actor=OLD_WOMAN
---

Now look for Antura! It went to the bakery for a baguette! #line:076ef0f 
Hurry up! #line:0e9c3e7

docs.title: baguette_salt
docs.text:
group: bakery
color: yellow
tags: actor=CRAZY_MAN
---
<<action COLLECT_1>>
<<card baguette_salt>>
This is salt. #line:00f1d2f

docs.title: baguette_flour
docs.text:
group: bakery
color: yellow
tags: actor=CRAZY_MAN
---
<<action COLLECT_2>>
<<card baguette_flour>>
This is flour. #line:06022b0

docs.title: baguette_water
docs.text:
group: bakery
color: yellow
tags: actor=CRAZY_MAN
---
<<action COLLECT_3>>
<<card baguette_water>>
This is water. #line:0c4d1f6

docs.title: baguette_yeast
docs.text:
group: bakery
color: yellow
tags: actor=CRAZY_MAN
---
<<action COLLECT_4>>
<<card baguette_yeast>>
This is yeast. #line:025865d

docs.title: talk_notre_dame_roof
docs.text:
group: notredame
tags: actor=OLD_MAN
---
<<asset notredame_fire>>
There was a big fire in 2019, but we could repair it. #line:09a0ead 
I saw Antura run into the Louvre Museum. #line:02ba888 
It is just across the River Seine. #line:00d22e5

docs.title: talk_louvre_guide
docs.text:
group: louvre
tags: actor=CRAZY_WOMAN
color: blue
---
Welcome to the Louvre Museum. What do you want to do? #line:0e6d2a5 
-> Tell me about the Louvre #line:0a5fc63 
    <<jump visit_louvre>>
-> Exit #line:0efc18f 
    <<if $MET_MONALISA>>
        <<action AREA_LOUVRE_EXIT>>
        Come back! #line:07dd921 
    <<else>>
        <<jump find_monalisa>>
    <<endif>>

docs.title: louvre_monalisa
docs.text:
group: louvre
color: yellow
tags: actor=CRAZY_WOMAN

The Eiffel Tower is 300 meters tall. #line:08c1973 
<<asset mr_eiffel>>
Built by Mr Eiffel in 1887. #line:09e5c3b 
<<asset iron>>
It's made of iron! #line:0d59ade 
I saw Antura going towards Notre Dame. #line:04d1e52 
<<camera_focus notredame>>
Get there! #line:083b3bf

docs.title: talk_eiffell_guide
docs.text:
group: toureiffel
actor: guide
---
<<if $TOTAL_COINS > 2>>
    Here is your ticket. #line:04e74ad 
    <<asset tour_eiffell_ticket>>
    <<set $TOTAL_COINS = $TOTAL_COINS-3>>
    <<action area_toureiffel>>
    <<asset tour_eiffell_map>>
    I saw Antura going up to the top of the tower. #line:089abda 
    Take the elevator! #line:0585a5e 
<<elseif $TOTAL_COINS > 0>> 
    Collect all coins! #line:04d966b 
<<else>>
    The ticket for the Eiffel Tower costs 3 coins. #line:069cbb3 
<<endif>>

docs.title: talk_notre_dame
docs.text:
group: notredame
tags: actor=OLD_MAN, asset=notredame
---
I'm the Mayor of Paris. #line:0cc11fa 
This is Notre-Dame Cathedral. #line:06f3fa2 
<<asset notredame>>
It is a famous Gothic church, built in 1182. #line:02edc0f 
<<action AREA_NOTREDAME_ROOF>>
Come with me up to the roof of the church! #line:083dfcc 
<<set $MET_MAJOR = true>>

docs.title: talk_cook
docs.text:
group: bakery
actor: CRAZY_MAN
---
<<if $COLLECTED_ITEMS >= 4>>
    GREAT! I can now bake the Baguette. And... #line:0017917 
    CONGRATULATIONS! You won the game! Did you like it? #line:0d11596 
    <<quest_end 3>>
<<else>>
    Help me! Antura made a mess in my kitchen! #line:07bbb10 
    I can't find the ingredients to make the baguette. #line:09e867c 
    <<asset  baguette>>
    Our special French bread! #line:0874503 
    <<set $QUEST_ITEMS = 4>>
    Please bring me 4 ingredients: #line:07d64c7 
    flour, water, yeast, and salt. #line:0c01530 
<<endif>>

docs.title: visit_louvre
docs.text:
group: louvre
tags: actor=CRAZY_WOMAN
---
<<asset louvre_inside>>
You can find many sculptures and paintings. #line:08dc97f 
<<jump find_monalisa>>

docs.title: find_monalisa
docs.text:
group: louvre
tags: actor=CRAZY_WOMAN
---

<<action monalisa>>
Go and find the Mona Lisa! #line:0442392

docs.title: go_bakery
docs.text:
group: louvre
tags: actor=OLD_WOMAN
---

Now look for Antura! It went to the bakery for a baguette! #line:076ef0f 
Hurry up! #line:0e9c3e7

docs.title: baguette_salt
docs.text:
group: bakery
color: yellow
tags: actor=CRAZY_MAN
---
<<action COLLECT_1>>
<<card baguette_salt>>
This is salt. #line:00f1d2f

docs.title: baguette_flour
docs.text:
group: bakery
color: yellow
tags: actor=CRAZY_MAN
---
<<action COLLECT_2>>
<<card baguette_flour>>
This is flour. #line:06022b0

docs.title: baguette_water
docs.text:
group: bakery
color: yellow
tags: actor=CRAZY_MAN
---
<<action COLLECT_3>>
<<card baguette_water>>
This is water. #line:0c4d1f6

docs.title: baguette_yeast
docs.text:
group: bakery
color: yellow
tags: actor=CRAZY_MAN
---
<<action COLLECT_4>>
<<card baguette_yeast>>
This is yeast. #line:025865d

docs.title: talk_notre_dame_roof
docs.text:
group: notredame
tags: actor=OLD_MAN
---
<<asset notredame_fire>>
There was a big fire in 2019, but we could repair it. #line:09a0ead 
I saw Antura run into the Louvre Museum. #line:02ba888 
It is just across the River Seine. #line:00d22e5

docs.title: talk_louvre_guide
docs.text:
group: louvre
tags: actor=CRAZY_WOMAN
color: blue
---
Welcome to the Louvre Museum. What do you want to do? #line:0e6d2a5 
-> Tell me about the Louvre #line:0a5fc63 
    <<jump visit_louvre>>
-> Exit #line:0efc18f 
    <<if $MET_MONALISA>>
        <<action AREA_LOUVRE_EXIT>>
        Come back! #line:07dd921 
    <<else>>
        <<jump find_monalisa>>
    <<endif>>

docs.title: louvre_monalisa
docs.text:
group: louvre
color: yellow
tags: actor=CRAZY_WOMAN
---
<<asset monalisa>>
This is the famous Mona Lisa. #line:louvre_monalisa_1
<<set $MET_MONALISA = true>>
<<asset leaonardodavinci>>
It was painted around 1500 #line:louvre_monalisa_2
by the artist and scientist Leonardo da Vinci. #line:louvre_monalisa_3

docs.title: louvre_liberty
docs.text:
group: louvre
color: yellow
tags: actor=CRAZY_WOMAN
---
<<asset liberty_leading>>
This painting represents freedom. #line:louvre_liberty_1
It's called Liberty Leading the People #line:louvre_liberty_2
by the French artist Eugène Delacroix #line:louvre_liberty_3

docs.title: louvre_venus
docs.text:
group: louvre
color: yellow
tags: actor=CRAZY_WOMAN, 
---
<<asset venusmilo>>
The Venus de Milo, an ancient Greek marble sculpture. #line:053d4fe

docs.title: npc_woman
docs.text:
tags: actor=WOMAN
color: purple
---
Ciao. What do you want to know? #line:0070084 
-> What is the Eiffel Tower? #line:0d91dc0 
    The famous iron tower, 300 meters tall. #line:0f17af0 
    The symbol of Paris! #line:07a113f 
-> Where are we? #line:09dd1da 
    We are in Paris, the city of love! #line:02b627d 
-> Is this place real? #line:08bede4 
    Sure! Why are you asking? #line:08654e6 
    Well... it looks like a video game, doesn't it? #line:0bc62a3 
-> Nothing. Bye. #line:0fe0732

docs.title: npc_man
docs.text:
tags: actor=MAN
color: purple
---
Do you have any questions? #line:07b94e9 
-> Have you seen Antura? #line:0f18ad3 
    => Yes! Talk to everybody and follow the lights! #line:0cf9b4e 
    => No. Who is Antura? #line:0f9dd62 
-> What are you doing? #line:002796f 
    => I'm going to work! #line:0fe4ff4 
    => I'm going to buy bread at the bakery. #line:05a38a8 
-> Where do you come from? #line:05eabcf 
    => I wasn't born in this country. #line:0635a6a 
    => From planet Earth. #line:0749690 
-> Goodbye #line:0ee51fc

docs.title: npc_kid
docs.text:
tags: actor=KID_MALE
color: purple
---
=> Hi! #line:0c4d9e4 
=> How are you? #line:032d401

docs.title: Paris ! (fr_01)
docs.text:
Quest Index - Language: english - french - polish - italian

Educators & Designers: help improving this quest!

Comments and feedback: discuss in the Forum Improve translations: comment the Google Sheet Improve the script: propose an edit here

Version: 1.00 Status: NeedsReview Location: France - Paris

voyagez dans Paris à la recherche d’Antura !

docs.title: Content
docs.text:
Subjects:

  • Culture (x20)
  • History (x11)
  • Geography (x9)
  • Art (x6)
  • Food (x6)

docs.title: Topics
docs.text:
• Eiffel Tower
  • Baguette
  • Notre Dame
  • Louvre

docs.title: Cards
docs.text:
Tour Eiffel Une haute tour, symbole de Paris. Construite en fer, elle mesure 300 mètres de haut. De là-haut, on peut admirer toute la ville.

Baguette française Un pain long et croustillant, l'aliment le plus célèbre en France. Les Français achètent des baguettes fraîches tous les jours à la boulangerie !

Notre-Dame de Paris Une magnifique cathédrale de Paris. Ses vitraux colorés racontent des histoires.

Persienne Un immense musée rempli d'œuvres d'art. C'est là que vit la Joconde.

Paris Paris, capitale de la France, possède une célèbre tour haute appelée la Tour Eiffel !

Île-de-France Région de France où se trouve la capitale Paris. De nombreuses personnes y vivent !

Gustave Eiffel L'homme qui a construit la tour Eiffel ! C'était un ingénieur passionné de construction en fer et il a créé la tour la plus célèbre du monde.

Incendie de Notre-Dame En 2019, un important incendie a endommagé la magnifique cathédrale Notre-Dame de Paris. De nombreuses personnes ont œuvré ensemble pour sauver cet important édifice.

Carte de la Tour Eiffel Une carte qui montre où se trouve la célèbre haute tour de Paris.

Billet pour la Tour Eiffel Un document spécial qui vous permet de visiter la célèbre haute tour de Paris, en France.

La Joconde Le tableau le plus célèbre du monde ! Une femme au sourire mystérieux, peinte par Léonard de Vinci. Elle semble vous suivre du regard.

Cathédrale Une église très grande et importante dans une ville.

Église Un bâtiment spécial où les gens se réunissent pour prier et chanter.

Code d'Hammourabi Une pierre sur laquelle sont gravées de très vieilles lois.

Couronnement de Napoléon (David) Un immense tableau représentant une cérémonie d’un empereur français.

Ascenseurs de la Tour Eiffel Ascenseurs qui emmènent les visiteurs jusqu'à la tour de fer.

Arc-boutant Bras de pierre à l'extérieur qui aident à soutenir les hauts murs.

Gargouille Une statue drôle ou effrayante qui fonctionne également comme une gouttière.

Pyramide du Louvre La pyramide de verre qui constitue l’entrée moderne du Louvre.

Serment des Horaces (David) Un tableau de trois frères faisant une promesse courageuse.

Rosace Un grand vitrail rond qui raconte des histoires avec la lumière.

Le scribe assis Une ancienne statue égyptienne d'une personne en train d'écrire.

Victoire ailée de Samothrace Une célèbre sculpture antique d'une déesse avec des ailes.

Exposition universelle (1889) La grande exposition universelle où la Tour Eiffel a été présentée.

  1. Learn about Eiffel Tower construction
  2. visit Notre-Dame architecture
  3. explore Louvre artworks
  4. Bakery and French baguette making with ingredient collection

docs.title: Flow
docs.text:
Part 1: La Tour Eiffel and Gustave Eiffel Mission: Climb the Eiffel Tower with the elevator.

Part 2: Notre Dame de Paris Mission: find the 3 pieces of the map Character: MAJOR

Part 3: Musée du Louvre and Mona Lisa Mission: A stealth mission in the Louvre at night to go to Mona Lisa Character: TEACHER

Part 4: La Baguette Mission: Learn the art of making the perfect baguette and discover its cultural significance in French society.

Version: 1.00 Status: NeedsReview Location: France - Parigi

viaggia per Parigi cercando Antura!

docs.title: Content
docs.text:
Subjects:

  • Culture (x20)
  • History (x11)
  • Geography (x9)
  • Art (x6)
  • Food (x6)

docs.title: Topics
docs.text:
• Eiffel Tower
  • Baguette
  • Notre Dame
  • Louvre

docs.title: Cards
docs.text:
Torre Eiffel Un'alta torre, simbolo di Parigi. È fatta di ferro e alta 300 metri. È possibile salire e ammirare l'intera città.

Baguette francese Un pane lungo e croccante, il più famoso in Francia. I francesi comprano baguette fresche ogni giorno dal panettiere!

Notre-Dame de Paris Una splendida cattedrale di Parigi. Le sue vetrate colorate raccontano storie.

Louvre Un enorme museo pieno d'arte. È lì che vive la Monna Lisa.

Parigi La capitale della Francia. Parigi ha una famosa torre alta chiamata Torre Eiffel!

Île-de-France Una regione della Francia dove si trova la capitale Parigi. Qui vivono moltissime persone!

Gustave Eiffel L'uomo che costruì la Torre Eiffel! Era un ingegnere che amava costruire in ferro e creò la torre più famosa del mondo.

Incendio di Notre-Dame Nel 2019, un grande incendio ha danneggiato la splendida Cattedrale di Notre-Dame a Parigi. Molte persone hanno collaborato per salvare questo importante edificio.

Mappa della Torre Eiffel Una mappa che mostra dove si trova la famosa torre alta di Parigi.

biglietto per la Torre Eiffel Una carta speciale che ti consente di visitare la famosa torre alta di Parigi, in Francia.

Monna Lisa Il dipinto più famoso del mondo! Una donna dal sorriso misterioso dipinta da Leonardo da Vinci. Sembra seguirti con lo sguardo.

Cattedrale Una chiesa molto grande e importante in una città.

Chiesa Un edificio speciale dove le persone si incontrano per pregare e cantare.

Codice di Hammurabi Una pietra su cui sono incise leggi molto antiche.

Incoronazione di Napoleone (David) Un enorme dipinto raffigurante la cerimonia di un imperatore francese.

Ascensori della Torre Eiffel Ascensori che portano i visitatori sulla torre di ferro.

Arco rampante Braccia in pietra all'esterno che aiutano a sostenere alte mura.

Gargoyle Una statua divertente o spaventosa che funziona anche come sgorgatore di pioggia.

Piramide del Louvre La piramide di vetro che costituisce l'ingresso moderno del Louvre.

Giuramento degli Orazi (Davide) Un dipinto raffigurante tre fratelli che fanno una promessa coraggiosa.

Rosone Una grande vetrata rotonda che racconta storie attraverso la luce.

Lo scriba seduto Un'antica statua egizia raffigurante una persona che scrive.

Vittoria alata di Samotracia Una famosa scultura antica raffigurante una dea con le ali.

Esposizione universale (1889) La grande esposizione universale in cui venne esposta la Torre Eiffel.

Version: 1.00 Status: NeedsReview Location: France - Paris

travel around paris searching for Antura!

docs.title: Content
docs.text:
Subjects:

  • Culture (x20)
  • History (x11)
  • Geography (x9)
  • Art (x6)
  • Food (x6)

docs.title: Topics
docs.text:
• Eiffel Tower
  • Baguette
  • Notre Dame
  • Louvre

docs.title: Cards
docs.text:
Eiffel Tower A tall tower and a symbol of Paris. It's made of iron and is 300 meters talll. You can go up and see the whole city.

French Baguette A long, crispy bread that is the most famous food in France. French people buy fresh baguettes every day from the bakery!

Notre-Dame de Paris A beautiful cathedral in Paris. Its colorful glass windows tell stories.

Louvre A huge museum full of art. It’s where the Mona Lisa lives.

Paris The capital city of France. Paris has a famous tall tower called the Eiffel Tower!

Ile-de-France A region in France where the capital city Paris is located. Many people live here!

Gustave Eiffel The man who built the Eiffel Tower! He was an engineer who loved building with iron and created the most famous tower in the world.

Notre-Dame Fire In 2019, a big fire damaged the beautiful Notre-Dame Cathedral in Paris. Many people worked together to save this important building.

Eiffel Tower map A map that shows where the famous tall tower in Paris is located.

Eiffel Tower ticket A special paper that lets you visit the famous tall tower in Paris, France.

Mona Lisa The most famous painting in the world! A woman with a mysterious smile painted by Leonardo da Vinci. She seems to follow you with her eyes.

Cathedral A very large and important church in a city.

Church A special building where people meet to pray and sing.

Code of Hammurabi A stone with very old laws carved into it.

Coronation of Napoleon (David) A huge painting showing a French emperor’s ceremony.

Eiffel Tower Elevators Lifts that take visitors up the iron tower.

Flying Buttress Stone arms outside that help hold up tall walls.

Gargoyle A funny or scary statue that also works as a rain spout.

Louvre Pyramid The glass pyramid that is the Louvre’s modern entrance.

Oath of the Horatii (David) A painting of three brothers making a brave promise.

Rose Window A big round stained-glass window that tells stories with light.

The Seated Scribe An ancient Egyptian statue of a person writing.

Winged Victory of Samothrace A famous ancient sculpture of a goddess with wings.

World’s Fair (1889) The big world expo when the Eiffel Tower was shown.

podróżuj po Paryżu szukając Antury!

docs.title: Content
docs.text:
Subjects:

  • Culture (x20)
  • History (x11)
  • Geography (x9)
  • Art (x6)
  • Food (x6)

docs.title: Topics
docs.text:
• Eiffel Tower
  • Baguette
  • Notre Dame
  • Louvre

docs.title: Cards
docs.text:
Wieża Eiffla Wysoka wieża i symbol Paryża. Jest wykonana z żelaza i ma 300 metrów wysokości. Można na nią wejść i zobaczyć całe miasto.

Bagietka francuska Długi, chrupiący chleb, najsłynniejsze danie we Francji. Francuzi codziennie kupują świeże bagietki z piekarni!

Katedra Notre-Dame w Paryżu Piękna katedra w Paryżu. Jej kolorowe witraże opowiadają historie.

Żaluzja Ogromne muzeum pełne sztuki. To tu mieszka Mona Lisa.

Paryż Stolica Francji. Paryż ma słynną wysoką wieżę zwaną Wieżą Eiffla!

Ile-de-France Region we Francji, w którym znajduje się stolica, Paryż. Mieszka tu wielu ludzi!

Gustave Eiffel Człowiek, który zbudował Wieżę Eiffla! Był inżynierem, który uwielbiał budować z żelaza i stworzył najsłynniejszą wieżę na świecie.

Pożar Notre-Dame W 2019 roku wielki pożar zniszczył piękną katedrę Notre-Dame w Paryżu. Wiele osób współpracowało, aby uratować ten ważny budynek.

Mapa Wieży Eiffla Mapa pokazująca lokalizację słynnej wysokiej wieży w Paryżu.

Bilet na Wieżę Eiffla Specjalny dokument umożliwiający odwiedzenie słynnej wysokiej wieży w Paryżu, we Francji.

Mona Lisa Najsłynniejszy obraz świata! Kobieta o tajemniczym uśmiechu namalowana przez Leonarda da Vinci. Wydaje się, że śledzi cię wzrokiem.

Katedra Bardzo duży i ważny kościół w mieście.

Kościół Specjalny budynek, w którym ludzie spotykają się, aby modlić się i śpiewać.

Kodeks Hammurabiego Kamień z wyrytymi bardzo starymi prawami.

Koronacja Napoleona (Dawid) Ogromny obraz przedstawiający ceremonię cesarza Francji.

Windy Wieży Eiffla Windy, którymi zwiedzający mogą wjechać na żelazną wieżę.

Latająca przypora Kamienne ramiona na zewnątrz, które pomagają podtrzymywać wysokie mury.

Gargulec Zabawna lub straszna figurka, która pełni również funkcję rynny deszczowej.

Piramida Luwru Szklana piramida, która stanowi współczesne wejście do Luwru.

Przysięga Horacjuszy (Dawida) Obraz przedstawiający trzech braci składających odważną obietnicę.

Okno rozetowe Duży, okrągły witraż opowiadający historie światłem.

Siedzący skryba Starożytna egipska statua przedstawiająca osobę piszącą.

Nike z Samotraki Słynna starożytna rzeźba przedstawiająca boginię ze skrzydłami.

Wystawa światowa (1889) Wielka światowa wystawa, na której pokazano Wieżę Eiffla.

docs.title: init
docs.text:
=
// Words used: school, classroom, backpack, lesson, CP, CE1, CE2, CM1, CM2, Collège, Lycée, Baccalauréat, geometry, cursive, canteen/menu
// activities in this quest:
// - find your classroom (task)
// - find your backpack (task)
// - match tools to shapes (geometry activity)
type: panel
tags: 
color: red
actor: TUTOR
---
<<set $MAX_PROGRESS = 10>>
<<declare $got_backpack = false>>
<<SetActive Collect_Backpack false>>
Welcome Angers! It's the first day of school! #line:014887e 
You are 10 years old and in the last year of elementary school. #line:063e8e0 
Find your school and your classroom! #line:0f65a1b 
<<task_start TASK_SCHOOL task_school_done>>

docs.title: task_school_done
docs.text:
tags: actor=TUTOR
---
TASK COMPLETED!  #line:03ed76a

docs.title: task_school_desc
docs.text:
type: task
---
Find your school! #line:0da284c

docs.title: school_1
docs.text:
tags: actor=WOMAN
actor: WOMAN
---
Welcome! This is a nursery school, for our youngest pupils aged 3 to 5. #line:096b721 
You are too big for our small chairs! Your school is nearby. #line:07ed07b

docs.title: school_2
docs.text:
tags:
---
<<if $got_backpack>>
    <<jump school_2_talk>>
<<else>>
    <<jump school_2_welcome>>
<<endif>>

docs.title: school_2_welcome
docs.text:
tags: actor=GUIDE
actor: GUIDE
---
Bonjour! Welcome! You've found it. This is your elementary school. #line:092b309 
It looks like you forgot your backpack. #line:0dd7977 
You'll need your books for the lesson. #line:044385f 
<<jump task_backpack>>

docs.title: school_3
docs.text:
tags: actor=KID_MALE
actor: KID_MALE
---
Hi! This is a middle school for ages 11 to 15. #line:06478e9 
You're almost old enough, but not yet. Keep looking! #line:0df97f8

docs.title: school_4
docs.text:
tags: actor=MAN
actor: MAN
---
Bonjour! This is a high school for ages 16 to 18. #line:04a0e4b 
Students study for the Baccalauréat exam here before university. #line:027eba1 
You're a bit too young. Try another school. #line:0630d14

docs.title: school_4_talk_man
docs.text:
tags: actor=OLD_MAN
actor: OLD_MAN
---
Hello! I found a backpack on my way here. #line:0c3b4fe 
These books are simpler than the ones my students use, #line:0d3aba1 
so I don't think it belongs to them. #line:09c5297 
<<SetActive Collect_Backpack>>
Could it be yours? #line:0f44535

docs.title: school_2_talk
docs.text:
tags: actor=GUIDE
actor: GUIDE
---
There it is! Let's go inside and start the lesson. #line:0624437 
<<action door_open_2>>
Come in! Your classroom is down the hall. #line:0b91268 
<<jump task_find_classroom >>

docs.title: task_find_classroom
docs.text:
tags: actor=TUTOR, task
color: green
---
TASK: Find your classroom. #line:058ddbc 
<<task_start TASK_CLASSROOM task_class_done>>

docs.title: task_classroom_desc
docs.text:
type: task
---
Find your class so you can start the lesson.  #line:00e71a9

docs.title: task_class_done
docs.text:
tags: actor=TUTOR
---
YES! You can now start the lesson. #line:093e91d

docs.title: classroom_1
docs.text:
tags: actor=MAN
actor: MAN
---
This is the CP classroom. #line:08b2774 
Children start primary school at 6 years old. #line:09bfe7f 
This is not your class. Try the next door! #line:068e48b 
<<action DOOR_OPEN_2>>

docs.title: classroom_2
docs.text:
tags: actor=WOMAN
actor: WOMAN
---
This is CE1, the second year of primary school. #line:08f3ab2 
This is not your class. Try again! #line:029fd9c 
<<action DOOR_OPEN_3>>

docs.title: classroom_3
docs.text:
tags: actor=WOMAN
actor: WOMAN
---
Hi there! This is the CE2 classroom #line:0bce01a 
for students who are 8 years old. #line:0b411ce 
This is not your class, but you're getting close! #line:0e4aa95 
<<action DOOR_OPEN_4>>

docs.title: classroom_4
docs.text:
tags: actor=OLD_WOMAN
actor: OLD_WOMAN
---
Hello! This is CM1, the second-to-last year of primary school. #line:0532330 
Your class is just over there! #line:00f6294 
<<action DOOR_OPEN_5>>

docs.title: classroom_5
docs.text:
tags: actor=GUIDE
color: purple
actor: GUIDE
---
There you are! Welcome to CM2! #line:02797f8 
Let's get to work. #line:0f90123 
Look, it's a writing lesson. #line:0071a0d 
In France, we learn to write in cursive. #line:0f8995f 
<<card concept_cursive_writing zoom>>
Now for geometry! #line:0365921 
<<jump activity_match_geometry>>

docs.title: activity_match_geometry
docs.text:
---
Match each tool to the shape it draws. #line:052d22a 
<<activity match_shapes activity_match_done>>

docs.title: activity_match_done
docs.text:
tags: actor=GUIDE
actor: GUIDE
color: purple
---
<<if GetActivityResult("match_shapes") > 0>>
Great job! #line:0e3f1fa 
Every morning and afternoon we have a break to play outside. #line:037391b 
You're all set. Welcome to the class! #line:021df05 
<<quest_end>>
<<else>>
Not quite right, let's try again. #line:0a5c8f1
<<jump activity_match_geometry>>
<<endif>>

docs.title: task_backpack
docs.text:
tags: actor=GUIDE, task
color: green
---
Find your backpack and then come back. #line:0e3ad75 
<<task_start TASK_BACKPACK task_backpack_done>>

docs.title: task_backpack_done
docs.text:
tags: actor=TUTOR
---
TASK COMPLETED! You can now enter school. #line:063f354  
<<set $got_backpack = true>>

docs.title: task_backpack_desc
docs.text:
type: task
---
Find your backpack so you can go to school.  #line:00faf2f

docs.title: school_canteen
docs.text:
tags: actor=OLD_WOMAN, 
color: yellow
---
Hello! I hope you're registered for the canteen. #line:021132d 
Look what's on the menu today! #line:0978619 
<<card object_canteen_menu>>

docs.title: school_charte
docs.text:
tags: actor=TUTOR
color: yellow
tags: item
---
<<card concept_charter_of_secularism>>
At school, everyone is welcome and respected, #line:02977c9 
no matter their beliefs. #line:09c12ac

Great job! #line:0e3f1fa

You're all set. Welcome to the class! #line:021df05 
<<quest_end>>
<<else>>
Not quite right, let's try again. #line:0a5c8f1
<<jump activity_match_geometry>>
<<endif>>

docs.title: Le système scolaire (fr_02)
docs.text:
Quest Index - Language: english - french - polish - italian

Educators & Designers: help improving this quest!

Comments and feedback: discuss in the Forum Improve translations: comment the Google Sheet Improve the script: propose an edit here

Version: 1.00 Status: NeedsReview Location: France - Angers

docs.title: Content
docs.text:
Subjects:

  • Culture (x9)
  • Education (x7)
  • Math (x7)
  • Community (x2)
  • Art (x1)

docs.title: Topics
docs.text:
• French School
  • About Math

docs.title: Cards
docs.text:
Écriture cursive Une façon particulière d'écrire où toutes les lettres d'un mot sont reliées entre elles. En France, les enfants apprennent cette écriture à l'école.

Cercle Une forme ronde sans coins. Les cercles ressemblent à des roues, des boules et des pièces de monnaie !

Doubler Une ligne droite reliant un point à un autre. Les lignes peuvent être longues ou courtes.

Triangle Une forme à trois côtés droits et trois coins. Les triangles ressemblent à des parts de pizza !

Boussole Un outil qui permet de dessiner des cercles parfaits. Il possède deux branches, comme des ciseaux.

Règle Un outil droit utilisé pour mesurer la longueur des objets. Les règles comportent des chiffres et des lignes.

Carré Un outil en forme de triangle utilisé pour dessiner des lignes droites et des angles droits en mathématiques.

Cartable Un sac que les élèves utilisent pour transporter leurs livres, crayons et devoirs à l'école.

Charte de la laïcité Un ensemble de règles visant à respecter les croyances de chacun. Il contribue à une coexistence pacifique.

Collège en France Collège en France pour les enfants de 11 à 15 ans. Les élèves apprennent de nombreuses matières et se préparent au lycée.

Menu de la cantine Une liste des aliments autorisés à la cantine. Elle vous aide à choisir vos repas !

Classe Une salle à l'école où les élèves s'assoient et apprennent de nouvelles choses de leur professeur.

Lycée en France Lycée en France pour les adolescents de 16 à 18 ans. Les élèves étudient dur pour l'examen du baccalauréat pour aller à l'université.

École maternelle École pour les petits de 3 à 5 ans. On apprend en jouant et en explorant.

École primaire en France École pour les enfants de 6 à 10 ans. Vous apprenez à lire, à écrire et à compter.

docs.title: Words
docs.text: • water

docs.title: Activities
docs.text: • Match

docs.title: Tasks
docs.text:
• [Interact] TASK_SCHOOL
  • [Collect] TASK_BACKPACK
  • [Interact] TASK_CLASSROOM

docs.title: Gameplay
docs.text:
• Difficulty: Normal
  • Duration (min): 20

docs.title: Credits

• Anne (France) (content)
  • Lucie Paillat (France) (content, design)
  • Stefano Cecere (Italy) (development)

docs.title: Game Design Notes
docs.text: The cat goes to different places until he finds his school. Then he visits the school and finds his class.

docs.title: Content
docs.text:
School system : the cats goes a lycée (16-18 ans), to a collège (11-15 ans), to a école maternelle (3-5 ans) and finally goes to an école primaire (6-10 ans), that corresponds to his age, called Jules Verne

A typical day at school

  • Morning childcare = garderie du matin
  • arrival at the school gate = le portail
  • work in class with different subjects/matières : maths, reading & writing
  • morning playtime (récréation) on the playground (la cour)
  • lunch at the canteen (la cantine) or at home (à la maison)
  • school work with different subjects/matières : PE = EPS (éducation physique et - sportive), hist/geog = histoire/géographie, science = sciences, arts= art
  • evening childcare = garderie du soir / homework support = aide aux devoirs

School levels with level names and age (CP–6 ans / CE1-7 ans / CE2-8 ans /CM1-9 ans / CM2–10 ans).

docs.title: Knowledge content
docs.text:
The French School System: Kids learn the names, age groups, and order of the main schools:

  • École Maternelle (3-5 years old)
  • École Primaire (6-10 years old) - 5 years total.
  • Collège (11-15 years old)
  • Lycée (16-18 years old)

The Baccalauréat: Kids learn that the Lycée prepares you for a final diploma called le Baccalauréat, which is needed for university.

Classroom Life: Kids learn that French students learn to write in cursive, have playtime (la récréation) in the morning and afternoon, and study things like geometry.

Cultural Values: Introduction to the principles of French public schools via the national motto and the Charter of Secularism.

docs.title: Cards
docs.text:
Scrittura corsiva Un modo speciale di scrivere in cui tutte le lettere di una parola sono collegate. In Francia, i bambini imparano a scrivere in questo modo a scuola.

Cerchio Una forma rotonda senza angoli. I cerchi sembrano ruote, palline e monete!

Linea Un segno dritto che va da un punto all'altro. Le linee possono essere lunghe o corte.

Triangolo Una forma con tre lati dritti e tre angoli. I triangoli sembrano fette di pizza!

Bussola Uno strumento che ti aiuta a disegnare cerchi perfetti. Ha due gambe come forbici.

Governate Uno strumento dritto usato per misurare la lunghezza degli oggetti. I righelli hanno numeri e linee.

Piazza Strumento a forma di triangolo utilizzato per disegnare linee rette e angoli retti in matematica.

Cartella Una borsa che gli studenti usano per portare a scuola libri, matite e compiti.

Carta della Laicità Un insieme di regole per rispettare le convinzioni di tutti. Aiuta le persone a vivere insieme in pace.

Collège in Francia Scuola media in Francia per ragazzi dagli 11 ai 15 anni. Gli studenti studiano molte materie e si preparano per la scuola superiore.

Menù della mensa Un elenco che mostra quali alimenti puoi mangiare a scuola. Ti aiuta a scegliere cosa mangiare!

Aula Un'aula della scuola dove gli studenti si siedono e imparano cose nuove dal loro insegnante.

Liceo in Francia Scuola superiore in Francia per adolescenti dai 16 ai 18 anni. Gli studenti studiano duramente per sostenere l'esame di Baccalauréat, che permette loro di accedere all'università.

École Maternelle Scuola per bambini dai 3 ai 5 anni. Si impara giocando ed esplorando.

Scuola primaria in Francia Scuola per bambini dai 6 ai 10 anni. Si impara a leggere, scrivere e contare.

docs.title: Cards
docs.text:
Cursive Writing A special way of writing where all the letters in a word are connected. In France, children learn to write this way in school.

Circle A round shape with no corners. Circles look like wheels, balls, and coins!

Line A straight mark that goes from one point to another. Lines can be long or short.

Triangle A shape with three straight sides and three corners. Triangles look like pizza slices!

Compass A tool that helps you draw perfect circles. It has two legs like scissors.

Ruler A straight tool used to measure how long things are. Rulers have numbers and lines.

Square A triangle-shaped tool used to draw straight lines and right angles in math.

School bag A bag that students use to carry their books, pencils, and homework to school.

Charter of Secularism A set of rules for respecting everyone's beliefs. It helps people live together in peace.

Collège in France Middle school in France for kids aged 11 to 15. Students learn many subjects and prepare for high school.

Canteen menu A list that shows what food you can eat at school lunch. It helps you choose what to eat!

Classroom A room in school where students sit and learn new things from their teacher.

Lycée in France High school in France for teenagers aged 16 to 18. Students study hard for the Baccalauréat exam to go to university.

École Maternelle School for little kids aged 3 to 5. You learn by playing and exploring.

Primary School in France School for kids aged 6 to 10. You learn reading, writing, and counting.

docs.text:

  • Culture (x9)
  • Education (x7)
  • Math (x7)
  • Community (x2)

docs.title: Topics
docs.text:
• French School
  • About Math

docs.title: Cards
docs.text:
Pismo odręczne Specjalny sposób pisania, w którym wszystkie litery w słowie są połączone. We Francji dzieci uczą się tego sposobu pisania w szkole.

Koło Okrągły kształt bez rogów. Koła wyglądają jak koła, piłki i monety!

Linia Linia prosta, która prowadzi z jednego punktu do drugiego. Linie mogą być długie lub krótkie.

Trójkąt Kształt z trzema prostymi bokami i trzema narożnikami. Trójkąty wyglądają jak kawałki pizzy!

Kompas Narzędzie, które pomaga rysować idealne okręgi. Ma dwie nogi jak nożyczki.

Linijka Proste narzędzie do pomiaru długości przedmiotów. Linijki mają cyfry i linie.

Kwadrat Narzędzie w kształcie trójkąta, służące do rysowania linii prostych i kątów prostych w matematyce.

Torba szkolna Torba, w której uczniowie noszą do szkoły książki, ołówki i prace domowe.

Karta świeckości Zbiór zasad szanujących przekonania każdego. Pomaga ludziom żyć razem w pokoju.

Collège we Francji Szkoła średnia we Francji dla dzieci w wieku od 11 do 15 lat. Uczniowie uczą się wielu przedmiotów i przygotowują się do szkoły średniej.

Menu stołówki Lista pokazująca, co możesz zjeść w szkolnym lunchu. Pomoże Ci wybrać, co zjeść!

Klasa Pomieszczenie w szkole, w którym uczniowie siedzą i uczą się nowych rzeczy od nauczyciela.

Liceum we Francji Szkoła średnia we Francji dla nastolatków w wieku od 16 do 18 lat. Uczniowie intensywnie przygotowują się do egzaminu maturalnego, aby móc iść na uniwersytet.

Szkoła Maternelle Szkoła dla małych dzieci w wieku od 3 do 5 lat. Uczysz się poprzez zabawę i odkrywanie.

Szkoła podstawowa we Francji Szkoła dla dzieci w wieku od 6 do 10 lat. Uczysz się czytania, pisania i liczenia.

docs.title: init
docs.text:
// FR_03 NANTES_MUSEUM - Jules Verne
// Tasks:
// - FIND_BOOKS (collect 4 Jules Verne books)
// - COLLECT_TRAIN (collect from "Around the World in 80 Days")
// - COLLECT_ROCKET (collect from "From Earth to the Moon")
// - COLLECT_SUBMARINE (collect from "20,000 Leagues Under the Sea")
// - COLLECT_BALLOON (collect from "Five Weeks in a Balloon")
// Words:
// writer, map, book, train, rocket, submarine, hot air balloon, science fiction, invention, exploration, adventure
tags: 
color: red
type: panel
---
<<set $TOTAL_COINS = 0>>
<<set $COLLECTED_ITEMS = 0>>
<<declare $QUEST_ITEMS = 4>>
Welcome to the Museum of Jules Verne in Nantes! #line:0b5e2f3

docs.title: talk_guide
docs.text:
tags: actor=CRAZY_WOMAN, asset=jules_verne_1
---
<<if $COLLECTED_ITEMS == 0>>
Welcome to the house of Jules Verne! #line:08f7bc1 
<<elseif $COLLECTED_ITEMS < $QUEST_ITEMS>>
<<jump task_find_books>>
<<else>>
<<jump won>>
<<endif>>

docs.title: task_find_books
docs.text:
tags: task
---
<<task_start FIND_BOOKS>>
<<asset jverne_mission_overview>>
Explore the house and find four of his books! #line:0aac249

docs.title: verne_painting
docs.text:
tags: actor=TUTOR, asset=jules_verne_1
---
This is Jules Verne. He was a writer. #line:096a3b3

docs.title: verne_house
docs.text:
tags: actor=TUTOR, asset=jules_verne_house
---
He was born in Nantes in 1828. #line:003b311

docs.title: map_nantes
docs.text:
tags: actor=TUTOR, asset=map_nantes
---
This is a map of Nantes. #line:09bcaba

docs.title: open_chest
docs.text:
tags:
---
<<if $COLLECTED_ITEMS >= 4>>
<<jump won>>
<<else>>
<<jump task_find_books>>
<<endif>>

docs.title: won
docs.text:
tags: actor=CRAZY_WOMAN
---
<<asset jules_verne_2>>
Great! You met Jules Verne, #line:099cdca 
the famous science fiction writer. #line:05a032e 
<<quest_end 3>>

docs.title: book_80days
docs.text:
color: yellow
tags: actor=TUTOR
---
<<asset book_80days>>
This book is "Around the World in 80 Days" #line:03131e3
<<jump train>>

docs.title: train
docs.text:
tags: item
---
<<asset train>>
This is an old train #line:0732ebc 
<<action COLLECT_TRAIN>>

tags: actor=TUTOR
---
<<card rocket>>
This is a space rocket. #line:0e5ae78

docs.title: book_moon
docs.text:
color: yellow
tags: actor=TUTOR
---
<<asset book_earthmoon>>
This book is "From earth to the Moon" #line:06df7d0 
<<jump paint_moon>>

docs.title: book_20000legues

docs.title: Jules Verne et les transports (fr_03)
docs.text:
Quest Index - Language: english - french - polish - italian

Educators & Designers: help improving this quest!

Comments and feedback: discuss in the Forum Improve translations: comment the Google Sheet Improve the script: propose an edit here

Version: 1.00 Status: NeedsReview Location: France - Nantes

docs.title: Content
docs.text:
Subjects:

  • Literature (x6)
  • Science (x5)
  • History (x2)
  • Space (x1)
  • Transportation (x1)

docs.title: Topics
docs.text: • Jules Verne

docs.title: Cards
docs.text:
Cinq semaines en ballon Un livre de Jules Verne sur la traversée de l'Afrique en montgolfière. Plein d'aventures et de découvertes !

Le tour du monde en quatre-vingts jours Un livre passionnant de Jules Verne sur les voyages autour du monde très rapidement en utilisant des trains, des bateaux et des montgolfières !

De la Terre à la Lune Un livre de Jules Verne sur la Lune dans un grand canon ! Il a été écrit avant l'existence des vraies fusées.

20 000 lieues sous les mers Un livre de Jules Verne relatant des aventures sous-marines à bord du sous-marin Nautilus. Vous y rencontrerez des créatures marines géantes !

Charte de la laïcité Un ensemble de règles visant à respecter les croyances de chacun. Il contribue à une coexistence pacifique.

Classe Une salle à l'école où les élèves s'assoient et apprennent de nouvelles choses de leur professeur.

Collège en France Collège en France pour les enfants de 11 à 15 ans. Les élèves apprennent de nombreuses matières et se préparent au lycée.

Affiche de l'école française Un grand tableau avec des informations qui s'affiche sur les murs des salles de classe dans les écoles françaises.

Montgolfière Un ballon volant à l'air chaud. Le premier a volé en France.

Jules Verne Un écrivain français qui a imaginé des aventures extraordinaires avant même qu'elles ne soient possibles ! Il a écrit sur les sous-marins, les fusées et les voyages autour du monde.

Fusée spatiale Une fusée qui va dans l'espace.

Sous-marin (Le Nautilus) Un sous-marin du roman de Jules Verne. Il pouvait explorer les profondeurs marines.

docs.title: Tasks
docs.text: • (none)

  • Duration (min): 15

  • Stefano Cecere (Italy) (development)

docs.title: Game Design Notes
docs.text:
Let’s go to Nantes and discover who Jules Verne was and discover some transport vehicles.

  • He was born in Nantes in 1828
  • He is a writer
  • He has written le tour du monde en 80 jours

Other books:

  • train & boat in Le tour du monde en 80 jours
  • hot air balloon in cinq semaines en ballon
  • submarin in 20.000 lieues sous les mers
  • rocket in de la terre à la lune

docs.title: Topics
docs.text: • Jules Verne

docs.title: Cards
docs.text:
Cinque settimane in mongolfiera Un libro di Jules Verne sul volo attraverso l'Africa in mongolfiera. È pieno di avventure e scoperte!

Il giro del mondo in ottanta giorni Un libro emozionante di Jules Verne che racconta come viaggiare velocemente intorno al mondo utilizzando treni, navi e mongolfiere!

Dalla Terra alla Luna Un libro di Jules Verne che parla di un viaggio sulla Luna a bordo di un enorme cannone! Fu scritto prima che esistessero i veri razzi.

20000 leghe sotto i mari Un libro di Jules Verne sulle avventure subacquee a bordo di un sottomarino chiamato Nautilus. Incontrerete creature marine giganti!

Carta della Laicità Un insieme di regole per rispettare le convinzioni di tutti. Aiuta le persone a vivere insieme in pace.

Aula Un'aula della scuola dove gli studenti si siedono e imparano cose nuove dal loro insegnante.

Collège in Francia Scuola media in Francia per ragazzi dagli 11 ai 15 anni. Gli studenti studiano molte materie e si preparano per la scuola superiore.

Manifesto della scuola francese Un'immagine di grandi dimensioni con informazioni appesa alle pareti delle aule delle scuole francesi.

Mongolfiera Un pallone che vola ad aria calda. Il primo esemplare volò in Francia.

Giulio Verne Uno scrittore francese che immaginava avventure incredibili prima che fossero possibili! Scrisse di sottomarini, razzi e viaggi intorno al mondo.

Razzo spaziale Un razzo che va nello spazio.

Sottomarino (Il Nautilus) Un sottomarino tratto dal racconto di Jules Verne. Poteva esplorare le profondità marine.

docs.title: Topics
docs.text: • Jules Verne

docs.title: Cards
docs.text:
Five Weeks in a Balloon A book by Jules Verne about flying across Africa in a hot air balloon. It's full of adventure and discovery!

Around the World in Eighty Days An exciting book by Jules Verne about traveling around the whole world very quickly using trains, ships, and hot air balloons!

From the Earth to the Moon A book by Jules Verne about going to the Moon in a big cannon! It was written before real rockets existed.

20000 Leagues Under the Sea A book by Jules Verne about underwater adventures in a submarine called the Nautilus. You meet giant sea creatures!

Charter of Secularism A set of rules for respecting everyone's beliefs. It helps people live together in peace.

Classroom A room in school where students sit and learn new things from their teacher.

Collège in France Middle school in France for kids aged 11 to 15. Students learn many subjects and prepare for high school.

French School Poster A big picture with information that hangs on classroom walls in French schools.

Hot-Air Balloon A balloon that flies using hot air. The first one flew in France.

Jules Verne A French writer who imagined amazing adventures before they were possible! He wrote about submarines, rockets, and traveling around the world.

Space Rocket A rocket that goes into space.

Submarine (The Nautilus) A submarine from Jules Verne’s story. It could explore the deep sea.

docs.title: Topics
docs.text: • Jules Verne

docs.title: Cards
docs.text:
Pięć tygodni w balonie Książka Juliusza Verne'a o locie nad Afryką balonem na ogrzane powietrze. Pełna przygód i odkryć!

Dookoła świata w osiemdziesiąt dni Ekscytująca książka Juliusza Verne'a o bardzo szybkiej podróży dookoła całego świata pociągami, statkami i balonami na ogrzane powietrze!

Z Ziemi na Księżyc Książka Juliusza Verne'a o podróży na Księżyc w wielkiej armacie! Została napisana, zanim jeszcze istniały prawdziwe rakiety.

20 000 mil podmorskiej żeglugi Książka Juliusza Verne'a o podwodnych przygodach w łodzi podwodnej Nautilus. Spotykasz gigantyczne morskie stworzenia!

Karta świeckości Zbiór zasad szanujących przekonania każdego. Pomaga ludziom żyć razem w pokoju.

Klasa Pomieszczenie w szkole, w którym uczniowie siedzą i uczą się nowych rzeczy od nauczyciela.

Collège we Francji Szkoła średnia we Francji dla dzieci w wieku od 11 do 15 lat. Uczniowie uczą się wielu przedmiotów i przygotowują się do szkoły średniej.

Francuski plakat szkolny Duży obraz z informacjami, który wisi na ścianach klas we francuskich szkołach.

Balon na ogrzane powietrze Balon latający na gorące powietrze. Pierwszy taki balon poleciał we Francji.

Juliusz Verne Francuski pisarz, który wyobrażał sobie niesamowite przygody, zanim stały się możliwe! Pisał o okrętach podwodnych, rakietach i podróżach po świecie.

Rakieta kosmiczna Rakieta, która leci w kosmos.

Łódź podwodna (Nautilus) Łódź podwodna z powieści Juliusza Verne'a. Mogła eksplorować głębiny morskie.

docs.title: init
docs.text:
// FR_04 LE_MANS - Racing & Road Safety
// Cards:
// - lemans_race (sports heritage)
// - race_car (automotive)
// - traffic_lights safety education)
// - stop_sign (safety education)
// - danger_sign (safety education)
// - zebra_crossing (safety education)
// - scooter (transportation)
// Tasks:
// - Experience Le Mans race as spectator
// - Navigate city streets following traffic rules
// - Identify and respond to traffic signs correctly
// - Reach train station safely
// Words used: race, car, speed, traffic, lights, stop, danger, zebra crossing, scooter, train station, safety, street, road, pedestrian, red, yellow, green, triangle, stripes, careful
tags:
type: panel
color: red
---
<<set $TOTAL_COINS = 0>>
<<set $COLLECTED_ITEMS = 0>>
Welcome to Le Mans! #line:0a4f3e1

docs.title: talk_friend
docs.text:
tags: actorUID=MAN
---
This is the race of Le Mans #line:02f370e 
Cars are very fast! #line:07f77f0 
Only adults can drive them. #line:0d0cd3f 
The race is finished. Go back home by train! #line:0b834f8 
Wow, those race cars are fast! #line:0e54cfc 
But remember, the racetrack is the only place to go that fast. #line:053328c 
On city streets, safety is what's most important. #line:0b70620 
It's time to go home! #line:06e4b9d 
Here, take my scooter to get to the train station. #line:00f2f65 
I'll wait for you there. #line:0425fb5 
Be very careful and pay attention to the signs! #line:0b5ee19

docs.title: sign_stop
docs.text:
tags: actorUID=TUTOR
---
This is a STOP sign. #line:09ac224 
It means you must always come to a complete stop #line:0fde84a 
and look both ways before you can go. #line:0758dbd

docs.title: restart_level
docs.text:
tags: actorUID=TUTOR
---
you didn't pay attention! retry! #line:0eed8ec

docs.title: sign_danger
docs.text:
tags: actorUID=TUTOR
---
This is a DANGER sign! #line:0a57038 
It warns you to be careful and slow down #line:02cfe75 
because there might be something unexpected on the road. #line:07915e7

docs.title: street_lights
docs.text:
tags: actorUID=TUTOR
---
These are traffic lights. #line:0355437 
RED - STOP #line:0cb35d6
YELLOW - SLOW DOWN #line:0b0c56c 
GREEN - GO! #line:0c87bce

docs.title: zebra_crossing
docs.text:
tags: actorUID=TUTOR, 
---
These white stripes are a zebra crossing. #line:02fbb57 
It gives pedestrians the right way. #line:085d81b 
So you must slow down when approaching it #line:01cd74f 
and stop if someone is trying to cross! #line:04e20a8

docs.title: danger_signs
docs.text:
tags: actorUID=TUTOR
---
They are TRIANGLE #line:08c3073

docs.title: bravo
docs.text:
tags: actorUID=MAN
---
=> You made it! #line:03b112c 
=> you followed all the rules perfectly. #line:0662662 
=> You're a street safety expert! Have a good trip! #line:0c88cfb

docs.text:
Subjects:

  • Safety (x6)
  • Education (x3)
  • Transportation (x3)
  • Health (x1)

docs.title: Tasks
docs.text: • (none)

docs.title: Gameplay
docs.text:
• Difficulty: Normal
  • Duration (min): 20

docs.title: Credits
docs.text:
• Anne (France) (content)
  • Lucie Paillat (France) (content, design)
  • Stefano Cecere (Italy) (development)

docs.title: Game Design
docs.text: After watching the famous "24 Hours of Le Mans" car race, help Cat navigate the city streets on a scooter to safely reach the train station, learning important traffic rules along the way.

docs.title: Flow
docs.text: The player must guide Cat on a scooter (trottinette) from the Le Mans racetrack to the train station. The path is linear but features four key intersections, each designed as a mini-game to teach a specific traffic rule (STOP sign, DANGER sign, traffic lights, zebra crossing). The player must successfully navigate these challenges while avoiding moving cars and bicycles to reach the destination.

docs.title: Un conte de deux châteaux (fr_05) - Script
docs.text:
Quest Index - Language: english - french - polish - italian

Educators & Designers: help improving this quest!

Comments and feedback: discuss in the Forum Improve translations: comment the Google Sheet Improve the script: propose an edit here 

// FR_05 CASTLES - A tale of two castles
// Cards:
// - chinon (historical castle)
// - chambord (renaissance castle)
// - chinon_defence (military architecture)
// - chambord_ball (royal culture)
// - bridge (castle architecture)
// - parapet (defensive architecture)
// - loopholes (military feature)
// - obj_helmet (knight equipment)
// - obj_sword (knight equipment)
// - obj_bow (knight equipment)
// - obj_armor (knight equipment)
// - obj_hat (noble attire)
// - obj_musical_instruments (court culture)
// - obj_dance_shoes (court culture)
// - obj_ball_mask (court culture)
// - map (regional geography)
// Tasks:
// - Collect knight items: helmet, sword, bow, armor
// - Collect prince items: hat, musical instrument, dance shoes, ball mask
// - Explore different castle functions (defense vs luxury)
// Activities:
// Words used: castle, Chinon, Chambord, drawbridge, parapet, loopholes, defense, knight, armor, sword, bow, helmet, prince, mask, shoes, map, room, interior, Loire Valley, medieval, royal, French heritage
//

docs.title: init
docs.text:
=
tags:
type: panel
color: red
---
<<set $COLLECTED_ITEMS = 0>>
Welcome to the castles of the Loire! #line:09dda7c

docs.title: talk_guide
docs.text:
tags: actor=GUIDE
---
<<if $COLLECTED_ITEMS >= 8>>
<<jump guide_question>>
<<else>>
Antura ran away and is hiding in a castle! #line:067c028 
<<endif>>

docs.title: guide_defence
docs.text:
tags: actor=CRAZY_WOMAN, asset=chinon
---
This is the castle of Chinon. #line:06eaf5c 
<<jump FR_05_CASTLES_Text_98_0a_8d>>

docs.title: guide_living
docs.text:
tags: actor=CRAZY_WOMAN, asset=chambord
---
Welcome to Chambord Castle. #line:0a06d3f 
<<jump FR_05_CASTLES_Text_91139071>>

docs.title: FR_05_CASTLES_Text_02040108
docs.text:
tags: actor=CRAZY_WOMAN
---
Find all the things a knight wears: #line:0bfad5e 
helmet, sword, bow, and armor. #line:08b44fc

docs.title: FR_05_CASTLES_Text_02082173
docs.text:
tags: actor=CRAZY_WOMAN
---
Find all the prince's items: #line:0cb22cb 
hat, musical instrument, dance shoes, and ball mask. #line:0d2be40

docs.title: guide_question
docs.text:
tags: actor=GUIDE
---
Did you find Antura? #line:01003d0 
-> "YES": #line:0d9f509 
    <<jump endgame>>
-> "NO": #line:0de6ebb 
    <<jump endgame>>

docs.title: endgame
docs.text:
tags: actor=CRAZY_WOMAN
---
<<asset map>>
Did you like the castles? There are 200 castles like this in the Loire Valley! #line:01e6614 
<<quest_end 3>>

docs.title: view_bridge
docs.text:
tags: actor=CRAZY_WOMAN, asset=bridge
---
A drawbridge is a kind of movable bridge used in castles. #line:0010896

docs.title: view_parapet
docs.text:
tags: actor=CRAZY_WOMAN, asset=parapet
---
A parapet has gaps used for defense. #line:0aaf4dd

docs.title: obg_helmet
docs.text:
tags: actor=TUTOR, 
---
<<card obj_helmet>>
a helmet #line:0b492f6

docs.title: obj_hat
docs.text:
tags: actor=TUTOR, asset=obj_hat
---
a hat #line:0806c77

docs.title: obg_sword
docs.text:
color: yellow
tags: actor=TUTOR, item
---
<<asset obj_sword>>
sword #line:0733b81

docs.title: obg_bow
docs.text:
color: yellow
tags: actor=TUTOR, item
---
<<asset  obj_bow>>
a bow and arrows #line:049d6e8

docs.title: obg_armor
docs.text:
color: yellow
tags: actor=TUTOR
---
<<asset  obj_armor>>
this is an armor #line:01ac543

docs.title: view_loopholes
docs.text:
color: yellow
tags: actor=CRAZY_WOMAN
---
<<asset loopholes>>
These are loopholes. They are used to shoot arrows at the enemy. #line:050e177

docs.title: FR_05_CASTLES_Text_08_89_98
docs.text:
tags: actor=CRAZY_WOMAN
---
<<asset  chinon_defence>>
This castle was used for defense. #line:026073b 
<<jump FR_05_CASTLES_Text_02040108>>

docs.title: FR_05_CASTLES_Text_98_0a_8d
docs.text:
tags: actor=CRAZY_WOMAN, asset=chinon_old
---
It is very old. #line:0a0e0ca 
<<jump FR_05_CASTLES_Text_08_89_98>>

docs.title: FR_05_CASTLES_Text_91139071
docs.text:
tags: actor=CRAZY_WOMAN, asset=chambord_ball
---
Kings and princes used this castle for balls, plays, and concerts. #line:07d0b04 
<<jump FR_05_CASTLES_Text_02082173>>

docs.title: obj_musical_intrument
docs.text:
tags: actor=TUTOR, asset=obj_musical_instruments
---
a musical instrument #line:07f4c19

docs.title: obj_shoes
docs.text:
tags: actor=TUTOR, asset=obj_dance_shoes
---
dance shoes #line:01ee90f

docs.title: obj_ball_mask

docs.title: Un conte de deux châteaux (fr_05)
docs.text:
Quest Index - Language: english - french - polish - italian

Educators & Designers: help improving this quest!

Comments and feedback: discuss in the Forum Improve translations: comment the Google Sheet Improve the script: propose an edit here

Version: 1.00 Status: NeedsReview Location: France - Loire

Voyagez dans la vallée de la Loire pour visiter deux châteaux très différents : une forte forteresse défensive et un magnifique palais royal

docs.title: Content
docs.text: Subjects:

docs.title: Cards
docs.text: Château de Chinon Le château de Chinon est une puissante forteresse de pierre perchée sur une colline surplombant la Loire. Jadis, des rois y séjournèrent, et Jeanne d'Arc vint y rencontrer le futur roi de France.

docs.title: Activities
docs.text: • (none)

docs.title: Tasks
docs.text: • (none)

docs.title: Gameplay
docs.text:
• Difficulty: Normal
  • Duration (min): 10

docs.title: Credits
docs.text:
• Anne (France) (content)
  • Fabio Cecere (Italy) (design, development)
  • Lucie Paillat (France) (content, design)
  • Stefano Cecere (Italy) (content, design, development)

docs.title: Game Design Notes
docs.text:
Journey to the Loire Valley and help Cat find the perfect outfits to visit two very different castles: a strong defensive fortress and a magnificent royal palace.

Medieval adventure exploring French castle heritage. Discover defensive architecture at Chinon and royal luxury at Chambord while collecting knight equipment and prince items. Learn about drawbridges, parapets, medieval life, and Loire Valley's 200+ castles.

Mission The player must visit two distinct castles on the Loire river. In each castle, they will embark on a scavenger hunt to find a complete outfit that is appropriate for that castle's function (a knight's armor in the defensive castle, a prince's finery in the pleasure castle). By finding the items, the player explores and learns the purpose of each castle's unique architecture.

docs.title: Knowledge Content
docs.text:
• Dual Function of Castles: To understand that castles aren't all the same; some are built for war (defensive), and some are built for living and parties (residential/pleasure).
  • Architectural Vocabulary (Defense): To identify and learn the purpose of a drawbridge, keep, ramparts, loopholes, and portcullis.
  • Architectural Vocabulary (Pleasure): To identify and learn the purpose of a royal bedroom (with canopied beds), formal gardens, a ballroom, and a facade with many large windows for light and views.

docs.title: Assets
docs.text:
• Environments:
  • A serene country environment representing the banks of the Loire River.
  • A detailed model of Château de Chinon, emphasizing its strong, defensive features (high walls, few windows, stone textures).
  • A detailed model of Château de Chambord, emphasizing its elegant, decorative features (large windows, ornate rooftops, gardens).
  • 3D Models
  • Knight's Outfit: Helmet, Armor, Sword, Bow, Arrow.
  • Prince's Outfit: Feathered Hat, Lute (musical instrument), Dancing Shoes, Masquerade Mask.
  • Sounds: River ambiance, castle soundscapes (echoing stone halls in Chinon, faint ballroom music in Chambord), a "quest item found" sound.

docs.title: Flow
docs.text:
The Mission: The quest begins on the bank of the Loire River. The Boatman is with his boat.

Boatman: "Bonjour! My friend Antura wants to visit the famous castles of the Loire, but he doesn't know what to wear! Can you help? First, take my boat to the fortress of Chinon and find the perfect outfit for a strong knight."

First Castle: Château de Chinon (Defense)

The player travels by boat to an imposing stone fortress. A sign at the entrance reads: "Château de Chinon: A Fortress for Defense." The "Knight's Outfit" checklist appears. * The player explores the castle to find the items, which are placed near relevant architectural features: * The Bow and Arrow are found near a loophole (a narrow window for archers). A pop-up explains: "Loopholes let defenders shoot arrows out, without letting enemy arrows in!" * The Helmet is on the parapet walk, high up on the castle ramparts. * The Sword is inside the central tower, the Keep. * The Armor is located just behind the portcullis (the main iron gate). * Bonus: At the top of the keep, the player can click on a catapult and launch a stone into the river. * Cutscene: Once all items are collected, a short, fun cutscene plays showing Antura proudly wearing the full suit of knight's armor. The Boatman's voice says: "Excellent! Now Antura looks like a real knight! Let's go to a very different castle: the magnificent Château de Chambord."

Second Castle: Château de Chambord (Pleasure)

  • The player travels by boat to an elegant, beautiful palace with many windows and gardens. A sign reads: "Château de Chambord: A Palace for Parties and Princes." The "Prince's Outfit" checklist appears.
  • The player explores the castle to find the items:
    • The Dancing Shoes are in the center of the grand Ballroom.
    • The Masquerade Mask is on a table in the castle's main courtyard where guests would arrive.
    • The Lute (musical instrument) is found in a luxurious room with ornate decorations.
    • The Feathered Hat is in the Royal Bedroom, next to a large canopied bed.
  • Cutscene: Once all items are collected, a cutscene shows Antura looking dashing in his full prince's outfit, complete with a hat and lute.

Conclusion: The player is automatically taken back to the Boatman on the river.

  • Boatman: "Bravo! You found the perfect outfits. Did you see how different the two castles were? One was a strong fort, and the other a beautiful palace. This region is famous for them - there are over 200 castles along the Loire River! You have a great eye for history!"

docs.text: See the full script here

docs.title: Cards
docs.text: Castello di Chinon Il Castello di Chinon è una possente fortezza in pietra su una collina che domina il fiume nella Valle della Loira. Molto tempo fa, i re soggiornavano qui e Giovanna d'Arco vi si recò per incontrare il futuro re di Francia.

docs.title: Game Design Notes
docs.text:
Journey to the Loire Valley and help Cat find the perfect outfits to visit two very different castles: a strong defensive fortress and a magnificent royal palace.

Medieval adventure exploring French castle heritage. Discover defensive architecture at Chinon and royal luxury at Chambord while collecting knight equipment and prince items. Learn about drawbridges, parapets, medieval life, and Loire Valley's 200+ castles.

Mission The player must visit two distinct castles on the Loire river. In each castle, they will embark on a scavenger hunt to find a complete outfit that is appropriate for that castle's function (a knight's armor in the defensive castle, a prince's finery in the pleasure castle). By finding the items, the player explores and learns the purpose of each castle's unique architecture.

docs.title: Knowledge Content
docs.text:
• Dual Function of Castles: To understand that castles aren't all the same; some are built for war (defensive), and some are built for living and parties (residential/pleasure).
  • Architectural Vocabulary (Defense): To identify and learn the purpose of a drawbridge, keep, ramparts, loopholes, and portcullis.
  • Architectural Vocabulary (Pleasure): To identify and learn the purpose of a royal bedroom (with canopied beds), formal gardens, a ballroom, and a facade with many large windows for light and views.

docs.title: Assets
docs.text:
• Environments:
  • A serene country environment representing the banks of the Loire River.
  • A detailed model of Château de Chinon, emphasizing its strong, defensive features (high walls, few windows, stone textures).
  • A detailed model of Château de Chambord, emphasizing its elegant, decorative features (large windows, ornate rooftops, gardens).
  • 3D Models
  • Knight's Outfit: Helmet, Armor, Sword, Bow, Arrow.
  • Prince's Outfit: Feathered Hat, Lute (musical instrument), Dancing Shoes, Masquerade Mask.
  • Sounds: River ambiance, castle soundscapes (echoing stone halls in Chinon, faint ballroom music in Chambord), a "quest item found" sound.

docs.title: Flow
docs.text:
The Mission: The quest begins on the bank of the Loire River. The Boatman is with his boat.

Boatman: "Bonjour! My friend Antura wants to visit the famous castles of the Loire, but he doesn't know what to wear! Can you help? First, take my boat to the fortress of Chinon and find the perfect outfit for a strong knight."

First Castle: Château de Chinon (Defense)

The player travels by boat to an imposing stone fortress. A sign at the entrance reads: "Château de Chinon: A Fortress for Defense." The "Knight's Outfit" checklist appears. * The player explores the castle to find the items, which are placed near relevant architectural features: * The Bow and Arrow are found near a loophole (a narrow window for archers). A pop-up explains: "Loopholes let defenders shoot arrows out, without letting enemy arrows in!" * The Helmet is on the parapet walk, high up on the castle ramparts. * The Sword is inside the central tower, the Keep. * The Armor is located just behind the portcullis (the main iron gate). * Bonus: At the top of the keep, the player can click on a catapult and launch a stone into the river. * Cutscene: Once all items are collected, a short, fun cutscene plays showing Antura proudly wearing the full suit of knight's armor. The Boatman's voice says: "Excellent! Now Antura looks like a real knight! Let's go to a very different castle: the magnificent Château de Chambord."

Second Castle: Château de Chambord (Pleasure)

  • The player travels by boat to an elegant, beautiful palace with many windows and gardens. A sign reads: "Château de Chambord: A Palace for Parties and Princes." The "Prince's Outfit" checklist appears.
  • The player explores the castle to find the items:
    • The Dancing Shoes are in the center of the grand Ballroom.
    • The Masquerade Mask is on a table in the castle's main courtyard where guests would arrive.
    • The Lute (musical instrument) is found in a luxurious room with ornate decorations.
    • The Feathered Hat is in the Royal Bedroom, next to a large canopied bed.
  • Cutscene: Once all items are collected, a cutscene shows Antura looking dashing in his full prince's outfit, complete with a hat and lute.

Conclusion: The player is automatically taken back to the Boatman on the river.

  • Boatman: "Bravo! You found the perfect outfits. Did you see how different the two castles were? One was a strong fort, and the other a beautiful palace. This region is famous for them - there are over 200 castles along the Loire River! You have a great eye for history!"

docs.title: Cards
docs.text:
Alpes Une haute chaîne de montagnes en Europe.

Sac à dos Un sac que vous portez sur votre dos.

Bobsleigh Un traîneau rapide utilisé pour glisser sur la glace.

Escalade Escalader des rochers ou de la glace avec un équipement spécial.

Manteau Une veste chaude pour le temps froid.

Crampons Poignées métalliques pointues que vous attachez aux bottes pour la glace.

Glacier Glace à mouvement lent trouvée sur les hautes montagnes.

Gants Des couvertures chaudes pour vos mains.

Chapeau Un bonnet chaud pour votre tête.

Randonnée Promenade sur des sentiers en pleine nature.

Glace Eau gelée qui peut être très glissante.

Marmotte Un animal de montagne à fourrure qui siffle.

Montagne Les piliers de la terre

Guide de montagne Une personne qui aide les gens à grimper en toute sécurité.

Corde Une ligne solide utilisée pour la sécurité lors de l'escalade.

Foulard Un tissu chaud que vous portez autour du cou.

Ski Glisser sur la neige avec des skis.

Neige Eau gelée qui tombe par temps froid.

Sommet Le sommet d'une montagne.

Soleil Une lumière vive qui peut se refléter sur la neige.

Lunettes de soleil Des lunettes qui protègent vos yeux de la lumière vive.

Vent Un air en mouvement qui peut sembler fort en montagne.
